description: US-11 Find and Reuse an Archived Record on Re-registration — Search the archive by name and date of birth and pre-fill the registration form. The returning applicant becomes a new customer with a new number; the archived record stays untouched.

userStories.title: Archive search by name and date of birth
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to find a returning applicant's archived record, so I do not retype a household FD already knows. Source: tasks/prd-us-11-reuse-archived-record.md §US-11.1
acceptanceCriteria: searchArchivedCustomers(deps, {lastName?, firstName?, birthDate?}) requires at least one criterion and rejects an all-empty query with a typed error
Name matching is case-insensitive and diacritic-tolerant (Müller/Mueller); the chosen approach is documented — a normalised search column is acceptable
Only ARCHIVED customers are returned, ordered most recently archived first
Each result carries name, date of birth, address, household size, the number they held, and the archive date and reason
Results are capped at 20 with a German 'refine your search' message rather than pagination
Repository query is indexed on lastName and birthDate
Integration test with two archived people sharing a last name, and one active customer that must not appear
Migration committed under prisma/migrations/ if the schema changed
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: draftFromArchived pre-fill
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a draft built from an archived record that creates and mutates nothing. Source: tasks/prd-us-11-reuse-archived-record.md §US-11.2
acceptanceCriteria: draftFromArchived(deps, {archivedCustomerId}) returns personal data, address and household members
The draft carries no customer number, no group, no card, no reminder count and no certificate
Test asserts the call creates nothing and mutates nothing
Test asserts household members are copied as new values — editing the draft cannot alter the archived record
Tested against hand-written fakes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Re-registration through the existing registerCustomer path
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want a returning applicant registered as a new customer, so the old archived record stays intact. Source: tasks/prd-us-11-reuse-archived-record.md §US-11.3
acceptanceCriteria: Registering from a draft goes through the existing registerCustomer use case — no parallel code path
Test seeds the situation where the old number is taken and asserts a different number is assigned
The new card index starts at 1 and the reminder count starts at 0, with the newly presented certificate recorded
Test asserts the archived record's status, number, cards and distribution records are unchanged before and after
A nullable self-referencing previousCustomerId is stored as display metadata that no rule ever reads
Migration committed under prisma/migrations/
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Archive search and pre-fill UI on the registration screen
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to search the archive during registration and pre-fill the form from a match. Source: tasks/prd-us-11-reuse-archived-record.md §US-11.4
acceptanceCriteria: The registration screen has an 'Im Archiv suchen' panel taking last name, first name and date of birth
Results show name, birthdate, address, former number, archive date and reason
Selecting a result pre-fills the form with every field still editable, and the screen states clearly that a new number and a new card will be issued
A visible control clears the pre-fill and starts blank
The former number is shown for context but never presented as the number to be assigned
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: E2E: re-register a returning customer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec proving re-registration reuses data without touching the archived record. Source: tasks/prd-us-11-reuse-archived-record.md §US-11.5
acceptanceCriteria: Spec registers a customer, archives them, then registers again via archive search
Spec asserts the household is pre-filled, the new number differs from the old one when the old one has since been taken, the card is k1 and the reminder count is 0
Spec asserts the archived record still exists with its own distribution history
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes
